Public Health Programs
Family Planning
Family Planning
Planning and spacing of pregnancies is an important part of reproductive health and overall well-being. Our services are free for anyone who financially qualifies. Sierra County Public Health offers confidential, no-cost family planning services to individuals who qualify based on income.
We Provide:
Well-person exams (including breast and cervical cancer screenings)
All FDA-approved birth control methods
Pregnancy testing and options counseling
Testing and treatment for sexually transmitted infections (STIs)
Reproductive health education and counseling
Services for both women and men of reproductive age
About Family PACT
Family PACT (Planning, Access, Care, and Treatment) is California's family planning program for low-income residents. It provides comprehensive reproductive health services at no cost to eligible individuals.
The program goals are to:
Promote optimal reproductive health
Reduce unintended pregnancies
Remove Financial barriers to accessing birth control and preventive services
The program fills a critical gap in health care for under-insured and uninsured Californians.
Who is Eligible for Family PACT?
You may qualify for Family PACT if you:
Are a California Resident
Have a family income at or below 200% of the Federal Poverty Level
Do not have other health insurance that covers family planning
Are medically eligible for family planning services
Medi-Cal clients with an unmet share of cost may also be eligible. Enrollment is completed on-site at the time of your visit.
All services are confidential and available regardless of immigration status.
What Services Are Covered:
Family PACT covers a wide range of reproductive health services, including:
Birth control (all FDA-approved methods)
Emergency contraception (Plan B)
Pregnancy testing and counseling
Preconception health counseling
STI testing and treatment
HIV screening
Cancer screenings (breast, cervical)
Male and female sterilization
Limited infertility services
Individual client reproductive health education and counseling is an ongoing component of all services.
